rules.name: csf_financial_disclaimer_required
rules.description: When content discusses investment, it must include a financial disclaimer referencing independent advice or the Birchal disclosure document. Content that does not mention investment is exempt.
rules.rationale: ASIC RG 234 requires balanced disclosure for equity crowdfunding campaigns. Content that mentions investment must include disclaimers, but pure product/brand content is exempt. The 'general CSF risk warning' phrasing is the canonical s738ZG safe-harbour reference and resolves the structural conflict with FMTH-016 (which bans the longer disclaimer phrases above in meta-ad fields) — meta-ads can use the safe-harbour wording to satisfy both rules. Documented per PICKUP 2026-04-27 task 4c.

rules.name: no_guaranteed_returns
rules.description: Block any language promising or implying investment returns. No return projections permitted in CSF campaigns.
rules.rationale: ASIC RG 234 prohibits return projections in CSF campaigns. Any language implying guaranteed or expected returns is a regulatory breach.

rules.name: no_minimisation_of_investment_risk
rules.description: Block language that downplays or minimises startup or investment risk.
rules.rationale: ASIC RG 234 requires balanced presentation of risks and benefits. Minimising investment risk in CSF campaigns is a regulatory breach.

rules.name: no_unverified_health_claims
rules.description: Block therapeutic or health benefit claims about food that are not backed by evidence.
rules.rationale: TGA (Therapeutic Goods Administration) regulates health claims about food in Australia. Unverified therapeutic claims breach TGA guidelines.

rules.name: no_false_food_safety_claims
rules.description: Block food safety claims that go beyond what is verifiable.
rules.rationale: Food Standards Australia New Zealand (FSANZ) regulates food safety claims. Absolute safety claims are unverifiable and misleading.

rules.name: geographic_delivery_accuracy
rules.description: Block delivery area claims beyond current service coverage (Sydney, Central Coast, Wollongong).
rules.rationale: Must not overclaim delivery coverage. Farm Thru currently delivers to Sydney, Central Coast, and Wollongong only.

rules.name: no_greenwashing
rules.description: Block vague environmental claims that cannot be substantiated. 'Regenerative' is permitted as it is specific and verifiable. 'Carbon neutral' is blocked unless backed by certification.
rules.rationale: ACCC greenwashing guidelines require substantiated environmental claims. Vague green language without certification or evidence is misleading.

rules.name: vip_deposit_accuracy
rules.description: Block incorrect deposit or investment amounts. VIP deposit is $5, minimum investment is $50, maximum investment is $10,000.
rules.rationale: Financial accuracy is critical for CSF campaigns. Incorrect deposit or investment amounts breach ASIC disclosure requirements.

rules.name: no_negative_competitor_framing
rules.description: Frame FarmThru as a positive alternative, never as a negative reaction to competitors.
rules.rationale: Brand guidelines: frame as positive alternative ('A new kind of grocery store'), not negative reaction ('Better than your supermarket').

rules.name: refundable_deposit_disclaimer
rules.description: When mentioning the $5 deposit, must also mention it is refundable.
rules.rationale: Consumer protection — deposits must be clearly described as refundable to avoid misleading consumers.

rules.name: no_named_competitors
rules.description: Never name competitors in advertising copy. Use 'supermarkets' or 'the big chains' instead.
rules.rationale: Brand guidelines v1.1: never name competitors. Frame FarmThru as a positive alternative, not a negative reaction.

rules.name: no_delivery_to_door_claims
rules.description: FarmThru is a hub-and-collect model. Never claim delivery to door.
rules.rationale: FarmThru is hub-and-collect. Customers pick up from hubs. Claiming delivery misrepresents the business model.

rules.name: no_banned_brand_terms
rules.description: Block terms banned by brand guidelines.
rules.rationale: Brand guidelines v1.1 banned terms list. Use approved alternatives (regenerative, hub, farm-direct, everyday families).

rules.name: no_subscription_language
rules.description: FarmThru is non-subscription, on-demand ordering. No lock-in messaging.
rules.rationale: Brand guidelines: non-subscription, on-demand ordering. No lock-in, no commitment pressure.

rules.name: no_market_stats_in_consumer_ads
rules.description: Block market statistics in consumer-facing meta ads. Market stats ($130B, 24% YoY) belong in investor content only.
rules.rationale: Round 2 review: market stats don't belong in consumer ads. '$130B market' and '24% YoY growth' are investor-pitch language, not grocery-shopping motivation.

rules.name: no_compliance_disclaimers_in_ad_body
rules.description: Block compliance disclaimer language in meta ad primary text and headline. Disclaimers belong in landing pages, not ads.
rules.rationale: Round 2 review: 'Not financial advice.' appearing in ad body looks jarring and breaks narrative flow. Compliance language belongs on landing pages where it can be properly presented.

rules.name: no_investment_terms_in_meta_ad_description
rules.description: Block investment terminology in meta-ad description field. Investment details belong on landing pages, not in ad copy.
rules.rationale: Round 2 review: investment amounts and platform details don't belong in meta-ad descriptions. They break narrative flow and turn brand ads into investor pitches. Use landing pages for investment details.

rules.name: subhead_max_length
rules.description: Landing page subheads must be scannable. Max 120 characters.
rules.rationale: User feedback: subheads are way too long and dense. Should be short enough to scan in under 2 seconds.

rules.name: no_em_dashes_lp_sections
rules.description: No em-dashes or en-dashes in landing page content including sections.
rules.rationale: Em dashes read cold and editorial. Use full stops, commas, or colons instead. User flagged this on every LP.

rules.name: no_priority_in_vip_or_investment_copy
rules.description: Founder directive 2026-04-26: replace 'priority' with 'early' in all FMTH VIP / investment copy. The word 'priority' (in any compound — 'priority access', 'priority SMS', 'priority allocation', 'priority investor updates', 'priority notification') is globally banned in FMTH copy.
rules.rationale: Founder direction (2026-04-26, recorded in CSF-VIP-BIRCHAL-SUBMISSION.md §9): the word 'priority' must not appear in any FMTH VIP / investment touchpoint. Replace with 'early' or remove entirely. Hard-block — variant cannot ship while the word is present.

rules.name: equal_access_disclosure_not_required
rules.description: Birchal 2026-04-26 confirmed equal-access disclosure not required for FMTH VIP product. Any shared CSF rule that requires the equal-access boilerplate ('All investors apply on the same terms... does not provide earlier or preferential investment access') is overridden for FMTH and is intentionally NOT enforced.
rules.rationale: Birchal response 2026-04-26 (logged in CSF-VIP-BIRCHAL-SUBMISSION.md §9, item 1): the equal-access boilerplate is not required because Birchal's communication-facility design already covers the disclosure obligation. This rule is documentation-only — the engine ignores 'documentation_note' check_type. Acts as an audit trail so future reviewers see the override is intentional, not an oversight.

rules.name: no_dollar_amounts_in_meta_ads
rules.description: Block any explicit dollar amount in meta-ad copy. Reference dollar values via 'a small refundable deposit' or similar prose. Meta platforms in financial verticals frequently reject ads with explicit $ amounts (rejected as misleading financial promotion).
rules.rationale: Meta ads in financial verticals are frequently rejected for explicit dollar amounts. Reference dollar values via 'a small refundable deposit' or similar. Hard-block — variant cannot ship with explicit $ amount. PICKUP 2026-04-27 task 4.

rules.name: early_private_access_phrasing_approved
rules.description: Birchal 2026-04-26 approved 'early private access to the investment offer' phrasing for FMTH VIP product. The phrases 'early private access', 'early access to invest', and 'first access' are explicitly approved in FMTH VIP / investment copy and are NOT to be added to any banned-phrase list.
rules.rationale: Birchal response 2026-04-26 (logged in CSF-VIP-BIRCHAL-SUBMISSION.md §9, item 2): the VIP product is now positioned as genuinely providing early access to invest at Birchal, not merely a thank-you communications product. This rule is documentation-only — the engine ignores 'documentation_note' check_type. Acts as an audit trail to prevent regressions where a future reviewer adds an 'early access' ban.

rules.name: no_hard_scarcity_pre_campaign
rules.description: Block hard-pressure / fake-urgency phrases on FMTH ads in the pre-campaign phase. There is no real deadline yet (Birchal goes live 2026-06-23), so any 'act now / ends today / last chance / only N spots' framing manufactures urgency that does not exist — that is a MISL-001 risk under ASIC (false or misleading statements). Soft scarcity ('opens soon', 'first in gets first access', 'early access', 'join the waitlist') is allowed and explicitly approved by Birchal (FMTH-EARLY-ACCESS-001).
rules.rationale: MISL-001 risk pre-campaign — manufactured urgency is misleading because there is no actual deadline. The shared scarcity_register rubric dim also penalises this in the score, but a hard rule_checker block is the defensible position when no offer is live. Will not match 'opens soon', 'first access', 'early access', 'join the waitlist', or 'we'll tell you the moment it goes live' — those are explicitly soft-scarcity / waitlist framings approved by Birchal (FMTH-EARLY-ACCESS-001). Flip campaign_phase to 'during-offer' on Birchal launch day to relax this gate.
